transcript: leaving their dreaming sister behind. While keeping her magic robes hidden, the cowherd offered his own garment as a substitute, and after gaining her trust, the pair began exploring the countryside. She was struck by his caring nature, and he learned to see the world's wonder through her eyes. Before long, the two had fallen deeply in love. The weaver and the cowherd built a prosperous life with their farm flourished, and the weaver taught her skills to local villagers. As time marched on, the pair was blessed with two healthy children, but their bull was growing old. Before he died, the bull implored the family to keep his hide and use its magic at their time of need. While the husband grieved for his friend, the weaver's mind turned to her other family. Dusting off her magical robes, the two returned home.
